title: Coq Cheat Sheet
description: Essential Coq commands and syntax for theorem proving.

commands.description: Start Coq interactive shell
Compile a Coq file
Check the version of Coq installed
commands.scenario: Check the version of Coq installed

commands.description: Apply a hypothesis or lemma
Introduce hypotheses into the proof context
commands.scenario: Introduce hypotheses into the proof context

commands.description: Simplify the goal

commands.command: Inductive nat : Type := | O : nat | S : nat -> nat.
commands.description: Define a simple inductive type

commands.command: Fixpoint add (n m : nat) : nat := match n with | O => m | S n' => S (add n' m) end.
commands.description: Define a function using pattern matching
commands.scenario: Define a function using pattern matching

commands.description: Use the 'destruct' tactic for case analysis
commands.scenario: Use the 'destruct' tactic for case analysis

commands.description: Set default editor for Coq
Add Coq to PATH environment variable
commands.scenario: Add Coq to PATH environment variable

commands.description: Configure Emacs for Coq development
Use 'induction' for structural induction
Use 'rewrite' to replace equals with equals
commands.scenario: Use 'rewrite' to replace equals with equals

commands.description: Use 'unfold' to expand definitions
A command-line tool to run Coq proofs in an interactive mode, useful for automated testing of proof scripts in CI/CD pipelines.
Use `Print Kernel.` to inspect the internal state of the kernel or `Print Term.` to see the exact term being checked, which is essential for debugging proof irrelevance issues.
Launch the Coq Integrated Development Environment (often via Emacs/CoqIDE) to provide a GUI-based feedback loop for real-time proof checking.
A tactic/command used to disable all printing optimizations, allowing you to see the full, un-simplified structure of inductive types and definitions during debugging.
Run Coq in batch mode to process a file and exit, useful for checking the correctness of a file without entering the interactive loop.
